name: Henry R. Godfriaux
motto: Virtus et Veritas

name: WILLIAM FRANCIS BELL
motto: Virtus et Veritas
description: The proud possessor of a near perfect "bay window."

name: CHARLES SPURGEON BILLINGS
motto: Virtus et Veritas; "Whoopee! Look at my honors."
description: Like a mule, he loves his melodious voice.

name: ASA Q. BURNS
motto: Virtus et Veritas
clubs: Junior Ready Speaking, Essay, Reading, and Oration Medals
description: An energetic little man Who gives an opinion whenever he can.

name: ERNEST FRANKLIN CANADAY
motto: "This is my beloved self in Whom I am well pleased."

name: ABEL CANTU
motto: "I believe in self-preservation— And myself—without reservation."
clubs: In Charge of Spanish at W. J. C., 1908

name: GLEN CLAIBORNE CARBAUGH
description: He ranks among the rankest.

name: WILLIAM BYRON CHARLES
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Mission Study Chairman '13-'14
description: So bristling with personality that he has escaped Attention by a very close shave—daily.

name: JOSEPH CLAXTON CLARKE
description: An ardent advocate of the back-to-the-farm movement.

name: REUBEN ISAIAH DAVIS
description: Perhaps a Prophet, perhaps a Rube.

name: JOHN RAY FILES
description: Like all files he is rough— And sharp—and rasps.

name: TOTA FUJII
description: This nice little Jap called Toto Does over his work much gloat.

name: JOHN SEWELL GEYER
clubs: Freshman, Sophomore, and Senior Basketball Teams '13, '14, '15
description: I cannot account for my rapid promotion as a society man unless it is because of my close associations with Dr. Sutherland.

name: GEORGE CONRAD GIESSING
description: Dear Brother Giessing All his teachers is fleecing.

name: HENRY RENILE GODFRIAUX
motto: "He goeth through the line of the foe like a ram."

name: ALVA WINFRED GRAMMER
clubs: President Board of Governors, New Ely
description: It's just board, board, board until we are all bored.

name: ARCHIE E. GROFF
motto: "With a short round body and a smiling grin He resembles the great God Billiken."

name: WILLIAM B. GROSS
description: "Stub" is so slow of movement that he gets up at 5:30 in order to make an 8:30 class. Ambition to run the Rock Island.

name: GEORGE ELTON HARRIS
motto: H. is quite a gun in debate As he trembles his voice and explains fate.

name: VICTOR D. HILL
description: This is the old Hill you have heard so much about.

name: LAIRD THOMAS HITES
motto: A familiar face will be gone from its place— Hites came A. D. I, and at last he is done.

name: HORACE McCLELLAN HUFFMAN
motto: Venus, O Venus, thy form is fair, But Venus, O Venus, please curry thy hair.

name: JOHN H. HUGHES
motto: "he has niz."
description: Big man, big mouth, long tongue but brains—"he has niz."

name: MILNOR JEFFRIES
description: Head Professor in Spelling. Fleet's pet.

name: JOSEPH ELWOOD LEWIS
motto: "By my refined manners and captivating eyes I will bring the world to my feet."
description: "By my refined manners and captivating eyes I will bring the world to my feet."

name: EARLE F. McCONNAHA
description: A scholar of the first magnitude.

name: NELSON EMERSON McCOY
description: A Junior who slipped into the Senior Class by reason of his age and grey hairs.

name: GRANT McGEE, Jr.
motto: "maketh a world happy with his poetry."
description: He "maketh a world happy with his poetry."

name: WALTER JEFFRIES MATHERLY
motto: "With all my imposing paraphernalia of colossal thoughts and deep thundering tones I will rock the ages with oratory."
clubs: Inter-Collegiate Debater for Four Successive Years
Leader Hon. William M. Fitch Debating Team 1914

name: CLARENCE JACOBS MILLER
description: A prominent nose He follows, wherever he goes.

name: LAWRENCE EMERSON NELSON
description: The biggest fool in school.

name: EARNEST A. PARTNEY
motto: "Never too broke to sport a ten cent smoke."

name: JOHN LAWRENCE PEPPER
description: A great man in his own estimation, but not a great yell leader in Dr. Cook's estimation.

name: LOWELL C. PINKERTON
description: Methinks me chin whiskers attracts the fair sex.

name: Pollard, J. Hybert
nicknames: J. Hydrant Pound A Long Pollard
motto: "J. Hydrant Pound A Long Pollard Thinks his unsound arguments ought to be follered."

name: Regan, Milton Robert
description: A face that is white as putty And a head that is woefully "nutty."

name: Robinson, J. L.
description: Reduced all his Math to English, has all the inclinations of a Son of Rest.

name: Rose, Joseph Norris
description: Instructor in "Hoss-swapping," "Champion Moonshiner" and eulogist of pretty women.

name: Scott, S. E.
motto: "It is not growing like a tree In bulk, doth make man better be."

name: Standeford, William Francis
motto: "Hath a lean and hungry look."

name: VICTOR EDWIN TATUM
description: This elegant, angelic Vic Has a smile that works with a click.

name: JOE PERKINS TURNER
motto: "When Joe you initiate Don't forget the size of his big .38."

name: ROGER J. WOLFE
description: An Oklahoma Wolfe, very tame.

name: FRANK HERBERT ROSE
description: Who relieved us of his presence early in the year.

name: JOEL ERNEST BRUNER
description: The extravagant blowing of Bruner Would fill the sails of a wind-driven schooner.

name: W. R. BALLENGER
motto: "I confess I am a mathematician."

name: R. W. BELL
description: I move slowly and surely in class room, in athletics all Missouri fears me.

name: J. C. BELT
motto: "Alas, my honors are burdensome."

name: W. M. BENNETT
motto: "I am sincere in my convictions."

name: C. D. BRANDOM
description: We never saw such noble fury in so small a man.

name: G. C. BRENNEMAN
motto: "Look cheerful upon me, God knowest I can't."

name: C. O. Brown
motto: "My desire is—"

name: Carlyle Campbell
motto: "My way is a silent way, I believe and work and pray."

name: W. J. Chase
description: Here's a fellow with an ugly face, a long, lean body, and surnamed Chase.

name: J. H. Clark
description: I am noted for my loud and boisterous ways.

name: L. C. Cook
description: I am a beautiful and modest lady's man

name: Earl Danly
description: I have never seen a greater miracle in the whole world than myself.

name: F. H. Davidson
description: I am slowly climbing the ladder of fame.

name: Arthur Davis
motto: "I seek a universal solvent."

name: F. J. Dieterle
motto: "Dutch ist recht."

name: R. H. Ewing
description: Handled 1000 pound mules to train for football.

name: L. W. Farrar
motto: "Silence is the perfect herald of joy."

name: G. O. Faulkner
motto: "Boldness is never blind; therefore it is not ill in judgment but good in execution."

name: E. A. FRANKLIN
motto: "I know everything, except myself."

name: A. L. HENDRIX
motto: "I am wondering at my advancement."

name: J. B. HIBBITS
motto: "Manners must adorn knowledge and smooth the way through life."
description: Manners must adorn knowledge and smooth the way through life.

name: L. H. HIBBITS
motto: "Behold! I am one of the twins."

name: E. A. HOOD
motto: "I have immortal longings in me."

name: P. L. JONES
description: I love to entertain the boys at the picture show.

name: J. D. KERN
motto: "Come, then, expressive silence, let me muse."

name: A. L. LANTZ
description: Refused to join the keen spitters.

name: H. G. LEEDY
clubs: K Σ; Excelsior; Sons of Rest; Fle Club; Inter-Collegiate Debater '11-'12.
description: A necessary adjunct of the State Legislature.

name: WM. F. LOTT
motto: "I live in a crowd of jollity not so much to enjoy company as to shun myself."

name: J. R. MANTEY
motto: "If I should BURST ...."

name: R. F. MARR
clubs: Dutch Club; Co-op Board; Basketball Class Team '13-'14-'15.
description: In all the silent manliness of life.

name: Miller, E. V.
description: Joy arises within me like a summer morn.

name: Newman, A. T.
description: I know everything, except myself.

name: Newport, V. J.
description: Motive in life—To sit on the back seat and sleep.

name: Ninegar, C. H.
description: Shall I bid her go? what if I do! Shall I bid her go and spare not? Oh! no, no, no, I dare not.

name: Potter, A. B.
description: It is the woman that seduces all mankind.

name: Price, G. V.
clubs: Lake Mohonk Essay on International Arbitration
description: A veritable Demosthenes.

name: Powell, E. J.
description: Impossible to be as important as he feels.

name: Ramsbottom, J. H.
description: Alas! I never dreamed She loved me.

name: Waldron, P. M.
description: Virtue is like a rich stone—best plainly set.
